<!-- Copy this file to projects/<slug>/prds/PRD-###-<feature-slug>.md and fill it in.
     For a SHIPPED feature (reconstructing after the fact), tag every claim
     [intended] / [inferred] / [unknown] — never invent intent or numbers. -->

# <Feature name>

## 1. Problem statement
<!-- What's broken, and for whom? One or two sentences. -->

## 2. Strategic rationale
<!-- Why this is worth building — why now. Tie it to the project's core value. -->

## 3. User story
<!-- As a [role], I want [capability] so that [outcome]. -->

## 4. Hypothesis / assumptions
<!-- Forward: "we believe X → Y; we're wrong if…"
     Retroactive: what had to be true for this to be worth building. -->

## 5. Impact & KPIs
<!-- (a) What the metric SHOULD be — the bet.
     (b) What we can measure TODAY — is it even instrumented? If not, say so.
     Never fabricate numbers. -->

## 6. Technical notes
<!-- How it's built (or would be) — services, data path, key libraries. -->

## 7. AI implementation notes
<!-- If AI is involved: model, prompt strategy, rough cost per call. Else: "n/a". -->

## 8. Testing plan
<!-- What's tested now vs. what should be. -->

## 9. Deferred / what we'd do differently
<!-- Forward: explicitly out of scope.
     Retroactive: what the build got wrong, over/under-built, or should revisit. -->
